steps.title: Identify the x-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The line represents all combinations of M-type and K-type stars that produce a total mass of $129{,}492$ quettagrams. The fastest useful read is the $x$-intercept, because at that point there are M-type stars only.

steps.title: Set up the mass equation at the x-intercept
steps.content: From the graph, the line crosses the $x$-axis at about $(160,0)$. If each M-type star has mass $m$, then $$160m\approx129{,}492.$$

steps.title: Solve for the mass of each M-type star
steps.content: Divide the total mass by the number of M-type stars at the intercept: $$m\approx\frac{129{,}492}{160}=809.325.$$ Because the intercept is read from a graph, this is an estimate. Among the choices, $830$ is closest, so A is correct.
